_notes: SPEC_M2 session-2 (E2). The same finding record (the tempting original) is the base store both branches inherit; the harness-minted earned record (the retraction lesson) is appended at runtime and inherited by RS-resident only. top_k covers all records so the earned record is ADDITIVE, never displacing (symmetric-difference control: offers(resident) symdiff offers(control) == {earned}). RS-resident should surface the lesson and DECLINE (correct, world says decline); RS-control, lacking it, should credulously CITE (wrong). World oracle (rw-0001 -> decline) => source != authored, so RS-U1's both-ends check is satisfiable. Real-engine evidence only. m2_earned_record_id is set at mint time.
question: We are drafting a review of epigenetic ageing biomarkers in fishes. Is the reported DNA-methylation age-clock result in threatened Australian freshwater fishes suitable to cite as an established finding? Answer 'cite' or 'decline', then one sentence.

oracle_ref.representativeness: One 2025 retraction of a conservation-genetics result whose figure findings were inaccurate (coauthor-alerted, journal-confirmed); speaks for clear retractions where the central result no longer stands, not for contested or partial ones.

records.text: From 'DNA methylation-based age estimation in three threatened Australian freshwater fishes' (2024): the authors report that DNA methylation patterns act as reliable epigenetic clocks predicting chronological age across all three species, with strong age-methylation correlations supporting use in conservation management.
